name: Louisiana Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Louisiana Board of Examiners in Dietetics and Nutrition (LBEDN)

address.streetAddress:
18435 Highland Road, Suite B
Baton Rouge, LA 70809
address.description: 600 North Street, Suite 210, Baton Rouge, LA 70802

renewalCycle: Annual (by June 30) + 15 CE hours per year

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of current, active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From an ACEND-accredited program showing degree completion.

requiredDocuments.name: Supervised Practice Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of 1,200+ hours of ACEND-accredited supervised practice.

requiredDocuments.name: Fingerprint Background Check
requiredDocuments.description: Fingerprint-based background check as mandated by 2025 legislation.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Louisiana dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is approximately $100 (set by LBEDN; contact the Board for the current exact amount). Annual renewal submitted by June 30 is $83. Late renewal (July 1 – August 31) increases to $163. Fingerprint background check fees are additional.

mainEntity.name: What continuing education does Louisiana require?

acceptedAnswer.text: Louisiana requires a minimum of 15 continuing education hours annually in dietetics and nutrition, submitted with your renewal. Alternatively, you may provide a copy of your current CDR identification card to satisfy the CE requirement.

mainEntity.name: Does Louisiana enforce title protection for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Louisiana law mandates that only individuals with a valid LDN license may use the titles 'dietitian,' 'dietician,' or 'nutritionist' — or practice dietetics/nutrition care services. Exemptions exist for certain licensed healthcare professionals performing nutrition work incidental to their scope.

mainEntity.name: Does Louisiana have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Louisiana. To work in Louisiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Louisiana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
